findings.title: Budget card "was X" badge converts the original amount to home currency, destroying the foreign figure it exists to show (no-rate currencies show the raw number with the wrong symbol)
findings.rootCause: Budgets.tsx:354 renders the original amount with `formatHome(b.originalAmount || 0, b.originalCurrency)`. `formatHome(amount, from)` converts `from`ãthe user's HOME currency and formats with the HOME symbol (currency.ts:137-141), discarding `b.originalCurrency` entirely. The badge's whole purpose (per the `originalAmount`/`originalCurrency` columns and budgets.py:89-96) is to preserve the user-typed foreign amount. The badge only renders when `b.originalCurrency !== 'EUR'` (line 353), so it is ALWAYS wrong when shown. For no-rate currencies `convertCurrency` falls back to rate=1 (currency.ts:71-78), so the foreign number passes through unscaled but gets the home symbol. The server-side PDF renderer gets this right ã it uses `tr.money(orig_curr, orig_amount)` (pdf/__init__.py:696-697) ã proving the intended behavior; the web card diverged.

findings.title: Create-budget modal shows "Budget saved" and keeps the optimistic row even when the server REJECTS the write (409 duplicate-scope / 4xx) ã row then silently vanishes on next sync
findings.rootCause: helpers.ts:408-411 does `await upsertBudget(budget); close(); showLiquidAlert(savedToast)` inside a try/catch. But `upsertBudget` ã `_upsertWithUpdatedAt` (api.ts:376) RETURNS a resolved `{ok:false,status:409|4xx}` on any non-2xx (it only throws nothing ã even the network catch at api.ts:433 returns `{ok:false}`). So the catch block (helpers.ts:412-417) is dead for server rejections; the success path always runs. The modal never inspects `result.ok`. This is the FE-2 "honest save" pattern that WAS applied to the expense form (ManualTab.tsx:473-474 checks `if (!res || !res.ok)`) but missed for budgets. On 409 the api layer also shows `errors.staleEdit` + calls `pullFromServer()` (api.ts:413-416), producing a double-toast and the vanishing-row desync.

findings.title: Account-wide ('all trips') budget is silently hidden and unrecoverable when the active trip is the only trip carrying budgets
findings.rootCause: On mount the filter lazy-initializes to the active trip when that trip has any budget: `filterTrip = activeTripId && allBudgets.some(b => b.tripId === activeTripId) ? activeTripId : ''` (Budgets.tsx:46-50). `visibleBudgets` then filters to an EXACT tripId match (line 52-53), excluding tripId='all' budgets. The filter chips (including the "All trips" reset chip) only render when `tripsInBudgets.length > 1` (line 83), and `tripsInBudgets` excludes 'all' (line 80-81). So with budgets = [{tripId:'t1'},{tripId:'all'}] and active=t1: filterTrip='t1', showTripChips=false ã the 'all' budget is filtered out with no UI to recover. `activeTripId` defaults to `STATE.trips[0].id` on every load (api.ts:201), so this state is common, not exotic.

findings.title: Overall card 'over-budget' threshold uses strict `>` while individual cards use `>=` ã same numbers read "Over budget" on the card but "Near limit" on the Overall
findings.rootCause: The per-card `budgetStatus` was tightened (audit B5) to treat hitting the ceiling as over: `if (target > 0 && spent >= target) tier='over'` (helpers.ts:200). The Overall tier was left at strict greater-than: `totalSpent > totalAllocated ? 'over' : overallPct > 80 ? 'near' : 'ok'` (Budgets.tsx:72-76). At exact equality (pct=100) the Overall falls through to the `>80` 'near' branch. The B5 fix didn't propagate to the Overall card.

findings.title: Read side hard-assumes budget.amount is EUR and ignores the stored currency column ã a non-EUR budget from the API/CSV-import/legacy path is misread as euros
findings.rootCause: The EUR-canonical invariant for budget.amount is enforced ONLY by the create modal (helpers.ts:395-401 converts to EUR and forces `currency:'EUR'`). The server (budgets.py:48-49) stores `amount` and `currency` verbatim with NO server-side conversion to EUR, and the read serializer ships both (data.py:1205-1222). But the frontend NEVER reads `b.currency` anywhere ã `allocatedAcrossBudgets`, `budgetStatus`, and both card renders all pass `b.amount` to `formatHome(..., 'EUR')`. So any write path other than the modal (the raw `/api/budgets` API, a future CSV import, or legacy data ã all explicitly acknowledged as live paths in budgets.py:62-63) can desynchronize amount/currency from the EUR assumption and be misread.

findings.title: Budgets page recomputes spend O(budgets û expenses) three times per render with no memoization
findings.rootCause: None of the aggregations are memoized. Per render the component computes `allocatedAcrossBudgets(visibleBudgets)` (O(budgetsôý) containment), `spentAcrossBudgets(visibleBudgets)` (O(expenses û budgets)), `overBudgetCount` which calls `budgetStatus(b)` (= `spentForBudget`, O(expenses)) for every visible budget (Budgets.tsx:67), and then the card map calls `budgetStatus(b)` AGAIN per card (line 301) ã a second full O(budgets û expenses) pass. The component subscribes to `expenses` (line 34), so every expense add/edit re-runs all of it. ~3û O(budgets û expenses) + O(budgetsôý) per render.
